---
title: "Random selection"
subtitle: "An R-driven workflow"
author: "Jacqueline Wu"
date: "2025-11-22"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

needed <- c("vroom", "dplyr", "stringr", "readr", "fs", "tidyr", "lubridate", "writexl")
to_install <- setdiff(needed, rownames(installed.packages()))
if (length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, quiet = TRUE)
invisible(lapply(needed, library, character.only = TRUE))
```
#### import CSVs and select columns
```{r}
path_hoh <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Hoh/clean_Hoh_ALL.csv"
path_cw <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Clearwater/clean_Clearwater_ALL.csv"
path_ew <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Ellsworth/clean_Ellsworth_ALL.csv"

df_hoh <- vroom::vroom(path_hoh, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
df_cw <- vroom::vroom(path_cw, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
df_ew <- vroom::vroom(path_ew, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
# How many rows dfs have before filtering
nrow(df_hoh)
nrow(df_cw)
nrow(df_ew)
```
#### Filter confidence level lower than 0.5
```{r}
df_hoh <- df_hoh |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(Confidence), Confidence >= 0.5)
df_cw  <- df_cw  |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(Confidence), Confidence >= 0.5)
df_ew  <- df_ew  |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(Confidence), Confidence >= 0.5)
# How many rows dfs have after filtering?
nrow(df_hoh)
nrow(df_cw)
nrow(df_ew)
# Keep necessary columns
df_hoh <- df_hoh %>% dplyr::select(site = study_site, plot = plot_number, aru = aru_number, date = record_date, hour = record_hour, species = common_name, start_s = `Start (s)`, end_s = `End (s)`, confidence = Confidence)
df_cw  <- df_cw %>% dplyr::select(site = study_site, plot = plot_number, aru = aru_number, date = record_date, hour = record_hour, species = common_name, start_s = `Start (s)`, end_s = `End (s)`, confidence = Confidence)
df_ew  <- df_ew %>% dplyr::select(site = study_site, plot = plot_number, aru = aru_number, date = record_date, hour = record_hour, species = common_name, start_s = `Start (s)`, end_s = `End (s)`, confidence = Confidence)
# save CSV
out_dir <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs"
fs::dir_create(out_dir)  

readr::write_csv(df_hoh, fs::path(out_dir, "clean_Hoh_0.5.csv"))
readr::write_csv(df_cw,  fs::path(out_dir, "clean_Clearwater_0.5.csv"))
readr::write_csv(df_ew,  fs::path(out_dir, "clean_Ellsworth_0.5.csv"))
```
#### Extract species by study site
```{r}
df_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  df_hoh,
  df_cw,
  df_ew
)

species_by_site <- df_all |>
  dplyr::distinct(site, species) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(site, species)

readr::write_csv(species_by_site,  fs::path(out_dir, "species_by_site_status.csv"))
```
#### test if species by site has any problems
```{r}
# check
path_check <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Hoh/Hoh_ALL_plots.csv"
df_check <- vroom::vroom(path_check, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
any(df_check$"Common name" == "American Robin")
any(df_check$"Common name" == "American Crow")
```
#### select random audio clips for checking
```{r}
path_hoh_all <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Hoh/Hoh_ALL_plots.csv"
path_cw_all  <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Clearwater/Clearwater_ALL_plots.csv"
path_ew_all  <- "E:/merge_all/Outputs/Ellsworth/Ellsworth_ALL_plots.csv"

df_h <- vroom::vroom(path_hoh_all, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
df_c  <- vroom::vroom(path_cw_all, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
df_e <- vroom::vroom(path_ew_all, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

clean_one_df <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::rename(common_name = `Common name`) %>%
    dplyr::filter(`Scientific name` != "Abiotic",
                  common_name != "Biotic Insect",
                  common_name != "Biotic Anuran") %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(Confidence), Confidence >= 0.5)
}
df_list <- list(df_h = df_h, df_c = df_c, df_e = df_e)
df_list_clean <- lapply(df_list, clean_one_df)
df_h <- df_list_clean$df_h
df_c <- df_list_clean$df_c
df_e <- df_list_clean$df_e

df_h <- df_h %>% dplyr::select(site = study_site, plot = plot_number, aru = aru_number, date = record_date, hour = record_hour, species = common_name, start_s = `Start (s)`, end_s = `End (s)`, confidence = Confidence, source = `source_file`)
df_c  <- df_c %>% dplyr::select(site = study_site, plot = plot_number, aru = aru_number, date = record_date, hour = record_hour, species = common_name, start_s = `Start (s)`, end_s = `End (s)`, confidence = Confidence, source = `source_file`)
df_e  <- df_e %>% dplyr::select(site = study_site, plot = plot_number, aru = aru_number, date = record_date, hour = record_hour, species = common_name, start_s = `Start (s)`, end_s = `End (s)`, confidence = Confidence, source = `source_file`)
# for each species, randomly sample up to 50 detections
sample_up_to_50_per_species <- function(dat) {
  # Split the dataframe into a list by species
  sp_list <- split(dat, dat$species)
  # For each species-specific df, sample up to 50 rows
  sampled_list <- lapply(sp_list, function(g) {
    n_rows <- nrow(g)  # number of detections for this species
    if (n_rows <= 50) {
      # If there are 50 or fewer rows, keep all
      return(g)
    } else {
      # If there are more than 50 rows, randomly sample 50
      idx <- sample.int(n_rows, 50) # random row indices
      return(g[idx, , drop = FALSE])
    }
})
  dplyr::bind_rows(sampled_list)
}
set.seed(123)
df_h_sampled <- sample_up_to_50_per_species(df_h)
df_c_sampled <- sample_up_to_50_per_species(df_c)
df_e_sampled <- sample_up_to_50_per_species(df_e)

readr::write_csv(df_h_sampled, fs::path(out_dir, "random_Hoh_50.csv"))
readr::write_csv(df_c_sampled,  fs::path(out_dir, "random_Clearwater_50.csv"))
readr::write_csv(df_e_sampled,  fs::path(out_dir, "random_Ellsworth_50.csv"))
```
#### advanced Ellsworth random selection
```{r}
# Get the list of species that appear in Hoh and/or Clearwater
species_other_sites <- union(unique(df_h$species), unique(df_c$species))

set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
sample_ellsworth_advanced <- function(dat, species_other_sites) {
  # dat: Ellsworth data frame (df_e), already cleaned and with a "species" column

  # Split the Ellsworth data frame into a list of data frames by species
  sp_list <- split(dat, dat$species)
  
  # Apply sampling rules species by species
  sampled_list <- lapply(names(sp_list), function(sp) {
    g <- sp_list[[sp]]  # data for one species
    n_det <- nrow(g)  # number of detections for this species in Ellsworth
    
    # Decide how many rows to keep for this species
    if (sp %in% species_other_sites) {
      # Species is shared with Hoh/Clearwater: keep up to 50 detections
      n_keep <- min(50L, n_det)
    } else {
      # Species appears only in Ellsworth
      if (n_det > 150L) {
        # More than 150 detections: keep 100
        n_keep <- 100L
      } else {
        # 150 or fewer detections: keep all
        n_keep <- n_det
      }
    }
    
    # If we are keeping all rows, no need to call sample()
    if (n_keep == n_det) {
      return(g)
    } else {
      # Randomly select row indices to keep
      idx <- sample.int(n_det, n_keep)
      return(g[idx, , drop = FALSE])
    }
  })
  
  # Combine all species-specific sampled data frames back into one
  dplyr::bind_rows(sampled_list)
}

# Apply the advanced sampling to Ellsworth (df_e)
df_e_sampled_advanced <- sample_ellsworth_advanced(df_e, species_other_sites)

df_e_sampled_advanced %>%
  dplyr::count(species, name = "n_sampled") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n_sampled)) %>%
  View()

df_e_counts <- df_e %>%
  dplyr::group_by(species) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_det = dplyr::n(), # number of detections
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(n_det))

# 5. Write the advanced Ellsworth sample to CSV
readr::write_csv(
  df_e_sampled_advanced,
  fs::path(out_dir, "random_Ellsworth_advanced_50_100.csv")
)

```
